pages.full_text:
We reached Oxford for lunch + Jenny gave us lovely pasta. We left [?Tinder] a went south to near the Channel Tunnel.

We spent the night at the Plough Stalisfield. The supper at 8 (booked head) was delicious. Rump of Kent lamb + cod Stalisfield Green. Before supper we walked 4 k along country lanes.
__Sunday April 23rd__ St. George's Day + French elections. No sign of elections. Le Cleree was closed at Reims. We parked in a square + walked to the cathedral. Lovely Chagalle window. He lived to 97. + stone carvings. Warm sunshine.

There's a Fair in the square. But I don't want to go on the rides. I must be getting old.

On TO a supermarket near Duc & Duc, an Auchan (robin). Drove to just beyond Brabant Revigny, a [beautiful] sur Ornain, a beautiful village campsite with running stream. Everything shut up, a free night. But oh so cold.
pages.notes: Page number not visible on this side (continuation from previous page). 'Le Cleree' is likely 'Le Clerc' (French supermarket chain). 'Duc & Duc' is likely 'Duc à Duc' (French supermarket). 'Brabant Revigny sur Ornain' refers to Revigny-sur-Ornain (Meuse, France). The word before 'sur Ornain' is crossed out (appears to be 'beautiful' written twice — once struck through, once kept). 'Sunday April 23rd' + St George's Day + French elections strongly matches 2017-04-23 (first round of French presidential election). '[?Tinder]' is unclear — could be a place near Oxford or a misread; could be 'Thame' or another town. 'booked head' in parentheses may mean the table was booked ahead.

pages.full_text: Reims. 1974. Chagall. Made in Reims Left lancet. Virgin + Child.
photo_captions: Reims. 1974. Chagall. Made in Reims Left lancet. Virgin + Child.
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a glued-in photograph of a Chagall stained glass window (blue-green tones, Virgin and Child). Caption written below the photo in blue ink. The '1974' in the caption likely refers to when the window was made/installed, not the diary date. Page number 97 visible in top-right corner.
